title: Parsons Defense & Critical Infrastructure — Insider Cluster Buy Post-Downgrade
catalyst: Between May 11 and May 14, 2026, four Parsons insiders — CEO Carey Smith and Directors Harry McMahon, Mark Holdsworth, and one additional — collectively purchased ~$2.1M of PSN common stock immediately after BofA cut its price target to $80. The cluster lined up with a string of contract announcements: a $2B U.S. Army Corps energy-resilience IDIQ slot, new architect-engineer work for the U.S. Air Force, and a $34M DTRA option-year exercise.
thesis: Parsons sits at the seam of three secular tailwinds the market keeps under-pricing: critical-infrastructure modernization (water, transportation, energy resilience), defense modernization (intelligence, cyber, nuclear), and federal supply-chain reshoring. The May insider cluster — into a falling stock — is the first time the CEO and multiple board members have all bought together since the 2024 contract trough, and it overlaps with the largest single contract win in segment history. The 12-month upside requires the federal infrastructure spend pipeline (IIJA + WRDA + DOD energy resilience) to continue accelerating, not just hold; if Q2 organic growth re-accelerates above the recent ~7% backlog conversion rate, the BofA-style multiple compression reverses.

source.title: Insider Buying: Harry McMahon Acquires Shares in Parsons Corp (PSN)

supporting_sources.title: PSN Rises As Parsons Lands $2B Army Energy Deal
Insider Buying: Mark Holdsworth Acquires 10,000 Shares of Parsons Corp (PSN)

what_would_promote: (a) Q2 2026 earnings showing organic-backlog book-to-bill above 1.2x AND federal-segment organic revenue growth above 10%, OR (b) confirmation of additional named contract wins above $500M in DOD energy resilience or intelligence segments, OR (c) the BofA / Goldman analyst community marking up estimates rather than down.
checks_remaining: (1) Need 1-2 primary sources beyond Form 4s and contract-win press releases (e.g., direct 10-Q references to backlog/book-to-bill); (2) need concrete entry/target/stop construction once the next print confirms direction; (3) need to compare against existing defense and infrastructure exposures (none currently in active opps — clean slate).

title: CDW — AI Hardware Pull-Forward Demand at the VAR Layer
catalyst: CDW reported Q1 2026 net sales of $5.68B (+9.2% YoY) on May 6, with management explicitly citing customers 'pulling forward hardware orders ahead of price increases and supply issues' driven by AI infrastructure modernization demand. On May 27, two CDW insiders cluster-purchased $2.5M of common stock — the first sizable insider buy since 2023.
thesis: Every framework we have for the AI buildout (chips, memory, grid, optics) skips the VAR layer that actually integrates and ships the gear to non-hyperscaler enterprises. CDW is the largest US technology solutions provider serving SMB, mid-market, education, and government — segments where AI deployment is just now starting, lagging hyperscaler capex by 18-24 months. The pull-forward demand language plus insider buying suggests Q2 visibility has firmed up; if the AI investment cycle is broadening beyond the Mag-7 customer set, CDW is the cleanest enterprise-AI proxy at a much more reasonable multiple than NVDA, AVGO, or AMD. Counter: CDW is a low-margin distributor with structural pricing pressure; the AI tailwind has to outweigh that drag for a sustained re-rating.

source.title: CDW Q1 Earnings Meet, Revenues Rise Y/Y on Infrastructure & AI Adoption

what_would_promote: (a) Q2 2026 print showing AI-attached hardware revenue growing >20% segmented and management raising FY guidance, OR (b) the insider buying continuing into June-July at higher dollar values, OR (c) channel checks confirming enterprise-AI deployments accelerating beyond the early pilot phase.
checks_remaining: (1) Need a primary source beyond IR press release — earnings call transcript with management's specific commentary on AI segment sizing and pipeline; (2) need to verify the cluster isn't options-related (Form 4 reading); (3) trade construction requires a view on whether the multiple compresses on margin pressure or expands on AI mix.
